quick_facts.label: Typical award and duration
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. in Interior Design is commonly offered as a six-semester, three-year undergraduate programme; however, programme structure is institution-specific, and some universities offer a four-year/honours format under their undergraduate framework.

quick_facts.label: Usual entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Examples of current university rules require Class 12 / 10+2 or an equivalent qualification. The required stream and minimum marks vary by institution: some accept any stream, while published thresholds in examples range from 45% for general-category applicants to institution-specific alternatives such as a relevant diploma.

quick_facts.label: Core learning areas
quick_facts.value: Published B.Sc. curricula combine design studios with spatial planning, interior materials and construction, furniture, lighting, building services, CAD/3D visualisation, drawings, costing, sustainability, project work and professional practice.

quick_facts.label: NATA relationship
quick_facts.value: NATA is the Council of Architecture’s aptitude test for admission to the first year of the five-year B.Arch. degree. It is therefore not a nationally prescribed admission test for a B.Sc. in Interior Design; a particular B.Sc. provider may set its own admissions process.

quick_facts.label: NIFT Entrance Exam relationship
quick_facts.value: NIFTEE is used for NIFT’s own B.Des., B.F.Tech. and postgraduate admissions. NIFT’s listed undergraduate design programmes include Fashion Interiors under B.Des., but this is distinct from a university B.Sc. in Interior Design.

quick_facts.label: Indicative published fee context
quick_facts.value: Fees are highly provider- and year-specific. For context, Sandip University publishes annual academic and special fees totalling ₹85,000 for its three-year B.Sc. Interior Design & Decoration, while AAFT College, Raipur publishes a total academic fee of ₹531,000 for its three-year B.Sc. Interior Design; applicants should verify the applicable intake-year fee notice and separate hostel, transport, application and refundable charges.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 12 is the usual entry point, but rules are set by the admitting university
paragraphs: A B.Sc. in Interior Design is not governed by one single national admission rule. Published institutional criteria show the usual baseline is a pass in 10+2 / Class 12 or an equivalent examination. Sandip University lists 10+2 in any discipline with at least 45% for general/open-category applicants and 40% for reserved-category applicants, with a Class 10 plus diploma route also listed. Thakur College of Science & Commerce similarly lists Class 12 or an equivalent qualification and states that it accepts applicants from any stream.

Applicants should treat the programme page, prospectus and admission notice for their chosen campus and intake year as controlling. They should check minimum marks, accepted boards, category concessions, portfolio or interview requirements, document requirements, and whether a diploma/lateral-entry route is available before applying.
bullets: Class 12 stream: may be open to Arts, Commerce and Science students, but confirm the provider’s rule.
Marks: thresholds differ; the examples cited publish 45% for general/open applicants, with a lower threshold for reserved categories at one provider.
Documents commonly needed in admissions workflows include Class 10 and 12 marksheets/certificates, identity and category certificates where applicable; exact requirements are institution-specific.

sections.heading: Duration: three years is a common B.Sc. format, alongside evolving four-year pathways
paragraphs: The canonical three-year framing matches programmes that are explicitly structured as six semesters: Sandip University lists six semesters and three years for its B.Sc. Interior Design & Decoration, and Bengaluru School of Design and Technology describes its B.Sc. Interior Design as a three-year, six-semester full-time course.

The degree title alone does not guarantee the same duration everywhere. Thakur College lists B.Sc./B.Sc. (Hons.) Interior Design as “3 Years / 4 Years,” and other providers advertise four-year B.Sc. pathways. This variation is consistent with the UGC’s undergraduate curriculum framework, which supports flexible, multidisciplinary undergraduate structures and multiple entry/exit options. Confirm the exit award, honours/research option, credits and total duration in the specific university regulations.
bullets: Common conventional route: 3 years / 6 semesters.
Possible alternative: 4-year honours or honours-with-research pathway.
Do not assume that a B.Sc. label has the same duration, credits or exit options across institutions.

sections.heading: Curriculum: studio design is supported by technical, visualisation and practice skills
paragraphs: Interior-design study is broader than decoration. Published programme content combines the creative work of conceiving spaces with technical knowledge needed to communicate and execute a project. AAFT’s B.Sc. outline includes basic design and visual arts, history of interiors, building materials and construction technology, residential and commercial space design, furniture design, execution drawings, estimating and costing, building services and sustainable interiors.

Digital visualisation and professional practice are recurring components. The same outline names AutoCAD, SketchUp, 3ds Max and Photoshop, while REVA describes design studios, core courses, electives, industry training and a final thesis/project, plus professional training under practising architects. Course names, software and the amount of internship/studio work differ by university, so candidates should obtain the current semester-wise syllabus rather than rely on a generic list.
bullets: Design foundation: visual arts, design thinking, interior history and space planning.
Technical development: materials, construction, furniture, lighting, building services, working/execution drawings and costing.
Digital and communication work: CAD, 3D visualisation, presentations and portfolio development.
Applied learning: studios, projects, industry exposure/training and, at some institutions, a final project or dissertation.

sections.heading: Admission: apply to the university; do not assume NATA or NIFTEE is required
paragraphs: For B.Sc. Interior Design, admission routes are set by the individual university or college. Some providers explicitly state that there is no entrance examination for their Bachelor of Interior Design route, while others may use merit, an institution-level assessment, an interview or other screening. The applicant should use the official admission notice for the particular campus and academic session to determine the process.

The two named parent examinations should be interpreted carefully. NATA is specifically a qualifying aptitude test for first-year admission to the five-year B.Arch. programme under the Council of Architecture framework, not a universal B.Sc. Interior Design test. Likewise, NIFTEE is the entrance examination for NIFT’s own programmes, including B.Des.; NIFT’s 2026 prospectus lists Fashion Interiors as a B.Des. specialisation, not a B.Sc. Interior Design degree. A NATA or NIFTEE score should only be submitted where the chosen institution expressly asks for it.
bullets: Start with the official programme page and current admission prospectus of the intended university.
Verify: eligibility, minimum marks, application form, entrance/portfolio/interview requirements, dates, reservation rules and fee schedule.
NATA: relevant to B.Arch. admission, not automatically to B.Sc. Interior Design.
NIFTEE: relevant to NIFT’s listed B.Des./B.F.Tech. and PG admissions, not automatically to a non-NIFT B.Sc. Interior Design course.

sections.heading: Costs: compare the complete, intake-year cost—not only tuition
paragraphs: There is no standard national fee for this degree. Published examples demonstrate substantial variation: Sandip University lists annual academic fees of ₹75,000 plus special fees of ₹10,000 for its three-year programme, whereas AAFT College, Raipur lists a total of ₹531,000 for its three-year B.Sc. Interior Design. These are provider-published figures available as of the review date, not a market average or a prediction of what another college will charge.

When comparing offers, separate tuition/academic fees from admission or prospectus fees, examination/library/lab charges, deposits, software or material costs, accommodation, transport and other living costs. Ask for the official itemised fee notice for the exact academic session and confirm refund, scholarship and instalment conditions in writing.
bullets: Published example 1: ₹85,000 per year in academic plus special fees at Sandip University for the cited programme.
Published example 2: ₹531,000 total academic fee at AAFT College, Raipur for the cited three-year programme.
These figures are dated provider examples and may change by session, campus, category, scholarship and payment plan.

sections.heading: Next options: employment, independent practice and postgraduate design or management study
paragraphs: A graduate can pursue design employment and build a portfolio through project, studio, retail, exhibition or site-execution work. Published B.Sc. career examples include interior/spatial designer, 3D visualiser, furniture designer, lighting designer and site supervisor. Related interior-architecture career material also identifies visual merchandising and set/scenography-oriented work as possible directions; suitability depends on the graduate’s portfolio, software skills, practical experience and the employer’s role requirements.

For postgraduate design study, CEED is one route to M.Des. admissions at participating design institutes. CEED 2026 permitted candidates with a degree, diploma or postgraduate programme of at least three years after 10+2, but qualifying CEED did not itself guarantee admission: institutes could impose their own eligibility checks, tests and interviews. Candidates considering an M.Des should check the precise discipline and eligibility at each target institute.

An MBA is another possible transition because management programmes admit graduates from different disciplines. For example, IIM Ahmedabad’s two-year MBA/PGP admission process uses CAT for domestic applicants and evaluates eligible applicants through its own selection process; its published pages describe students from arts, commerce, science and professional streams. CAT, CEED and postgraduate programme deadlines, eligibility and selection rules are revised by the relevant authorities and should be checked for the intended admission cycle.
bullets: Direct work and entrepreneurship: interior/spatial design, visualisation, furniture or lighting-related roles, site supervision, and eventually independent studio/firm work.
M.Des: CEED may be a relevant qualifying examination, but each institute separately decides final eligibility and selection.
M.Sc. in Interior Design/Interior Architecture: availability, title and eligibility are university-specific; verify the target programme directly.
MBA: a B.Sc. graduate may pursue management study; CAT-based selection requirements are set by individual IIMs and programmes.
